verdict.summary: RPC was cancelled by the client. Parent context cancelled, user navigated away, or explicit cancellation.

dead_ends.action: Retry cancelled RPCs unconditionally on the client side
dead_ends.why_fails: If the client explicitly cancelled (e.g., user action), retrying wastes resources and may cause duplicate side effects.

dead_ends.action: Ignore cancellation errors in the server handler
dead_ends.why_fails: Ignoring cancellation means the server continues expensive work that nobody will read. Always check context.is_active().

workarounds.action: Handle cancellation gracefully on the server side
workarounds.how: Check context.is_active() in long-running handlers; abort work early when cancelled to free resources

workarounds.action: Distinguish intentional vs. unintentional cancellation on client
workarounds.how: Track whether cancellation came from user action (no retry) or parent context (possible retry)

workarounds.action: Use context.add_callback() to clean up resources on cancellation
workarounds.how: context.add_callback(cleanup_fn)  # Python server; runs when RPC is cancelled
